reasoning: This is a pure copy kernel with zero arithmetic operations — it only reads from `in` and writes to `out`, yielding an arithmetic intensity of 0 FLOP/byte, far below the ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. The kernel moves 2 × 256 MB of data (one read + one write) with no computation, so its throughput is entirely determined by DRAM bandwidth. With coalesced access patterns and sufficient grid size (4096 × 256 = 1M threads covering 64M elements), DRAM utilization will be high, confirming a classic memory-bandwidth-limited scenario.
